markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define local, cloud, operator, validator, indexer, editor, and system lanes with actor IDs and capability constraints. This MT belongs to ParallelSwarmStateRecovery. Make WP-009 safe for local/cloud swarm work, backend navigation, and repeated session recovery.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Local and cloud swarm lanes must be observable, attributable, bounded, recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Every MT must leave enough receipts, state markers, and next-step context for session-limit recovery by a no-context model.
scope.expected_behavior: AgentLaneModel leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: AgentLaneModel is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

commits.slice: Parallel swarm state recovery hardening for MT-209..216

evidence.summary: MT-209..216 implement lane/capability model, claim/lease receipts, mailbox/handoff/checkpoint/recovery atomicity, recovery/reclaim behavior, attribution scrub, and EventLedger-backed state recovery surfaces.

commands.result: PASS; 16/16 parallel swarm state recovery tests passed
PASS; test target registered without duplicate names

evidence.summary: Reviewer passed MT-209..216 after capability, lease, mailbox, receipt, handoff/checkpoint/recovery, and claim/release atomicity hardening.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-209 requires defining local, cloud, operator, validator, indexer, editor, and system agent lanes with actor IDs and per-lane capability constraints, made observable/attributable/exclusive and PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed for parallel swarm work and session recovery. The code delivers a real typed AgentLaneKind/AgentCapability/AgentLaneIdentity model with per-lane capability sets, KernelActor mapping, secret scrubbing, and capability enforcement wired into real claim/mailbox/checkpoint/recovery paths, proven against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL with EventLedger receipts.
findings: AgentLaneKind enum (state_recovery.rs:57-66) defines all required lanes: Local, Cloud, Operator, Validator, IntegrationValidator, Indexer, Editor, System, with bidirectional string parse/as_str (lines 68-97).

Per-lane capability constraints implemented in AgentLaneIdentity::capabilities() (state_recovery.rs:206-271): Cloud deliberately excludes ClaimWorktree and WriteLocalIndex; Validator/IntegrationValidator restricted to InspectEvidence+NavigateBackend; Editor restricted to workspace/document/graph editing. Identity carries actor_id with safe-token validation (lines 187-204).

Capability enforcement is wired into live behavior via require_capability() (state_recovery.rs:5116-5128) called from real claim, mailbox handoff, navigation, checkpoint, recovery, and index-lease paths (lines 1346, 1486, 2222, 2404, 2471, 2855, 2938, 3013, 3071, 3557). KernelActor mapping (lines 282-294) attributes lanes to Operator/ValidationRunner/System/ModelAdapter.

Durable authority is real PostgreSQL: migration 0311_parallel_swarm_state_recovery.sql creates knowledge_agent_worktree_claims and related tables with FK references to kernel_event_ledger and a partial unique index enforcing one active claim per scope (lines 30-32), plus schema_registry rows.

Proof is real-PG, not mock: tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:108-165 builds isolated PG schemas on Handshake-managed PostgreSQL (managed_postgres::ManagedPostgres) or env URL with explicit NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback (lines 21,39-64); recovery_store() (tests:174) runs real migrations.

Test agent_lanes_and_work_claims_are_typed_attributable_and_exclusive (tests:776-875) exercises typed lane capability sets, asserts cloud lane lacks WriteLocalIndex, verifies secret scrubbing in serialized attribution, and proves exclusive claim arbitration + EventLedger KE- release receipts via sqlx queries against the real knowledge_agent_worktree_claims table.
No deferred-live escapes/placeholders: grep for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable variants in state_recovery.rs returned zero hits.
Aside (non-blocking, expected per ground rules): packet cites v02.192 anchors while current spec is v02.194; same section numbers resolve; spec drift is paperwork-only and not a failing reason.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
